text.title: API Monitoring — Health Checks Procedure
text.summary: 4-step procedure to apply API Monitoring — Health Checks end-to-end. Each step has named input, action, and output. Decision gates reference rules from `01-core-rules.xml`.

step.name: expose-two-endpoints
step.action: Add `/healthz` (no downstream calls) and `/readyz` (bounded downstream checks) endpoints; both return JSON with `status` and `checks[]` fields.

step.name: wire-k8s-probes
step.action: Configure liveness=/healthz, readiness=/readyz, startup=/healthz (or equivalent); periodSeconds 10s, failureThreshold 3.

step.name: verify-probe-latency
step.action: Measure probe p99 latency; refuse to ship if `/healthz` p99 >200ms (r4).

step.name: validate-and-monitor
step.action: Run `scripts/validate-api-monitoring-health-checks.py --self-test`; expose probe metrics so unreadiness shows up in dashboards.

section.title: Decision gates
section.p: Between steps, consult `content/06-decision-tree.xml` if the observed signal does not match expected output. The decision tree always terminates at a rule id from `01-core-rules.xml` or at `skip-this-methodology`.
